revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[azb:فوجین]][[de:Fūjin]][[en:Fūjin]][[es:Fūjin]][[fa:فوجین]][[fi:Fūjin]][[fr:Fūjin (divinité)]][[hy:Ֆուջին]][[id:Fūjin]][[it:Fūjin]][[ja:風神]][[ka:ფუჯინი]][[mni:ꯐꯨꯖꯤꯟ]][[pt:Fujin]][[ru:Фудзин]][[th:ฟูจิง]][[tr:Fūjin]][[uk:Бог вітру (Японія)]][[vi:Fūjin]][[zh:風神 (日本)]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Wind-God-Fujin-by-Ogata-Korin.jpg|venerated_in={{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}}} {{Short description|Japanese god of the wind}} {{other uses|Fujin (disambiguation)}} {{More citations needed|date=April 2018}} temple in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}.
{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, 13th century]] {{nihongo|'''Fūjin'''|||{{small|lit.}} "Wind God"}} or {{nihongo|'''Fūten'''|||{{small|lit.}} "Heavenly Wind"}}, sometimes also known as Ryobu, is the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese god of the {{ill|Wind|en|Wind|qid=Q8094|lt=Wind|1=Wind}} and one of the eldest [[Shinto]] gods.{{Cite book |last=Roberts |first=Jeremy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=xdfgjV2kw6oC&dq=fujin+god&pg=PP1 |title=Japanese Mythology A to Z |date=2009 |publisher=Infobase Publishing |isbn=978-1-4381-2802-3 |language=en}}{{Cite journal |date=2014 |title=Some Similar and Parallel Points Between the Turkic Legendary "Creation" and Similar Texts of Japan |url=https://www.ceeol.com/search/article-detail?id=532170 |journal=Folklor/Edebiyat |language=English |volume=20 |issue=77 |pages=189–196 |issn=1300-7491}} He is portrayed as a terrifying wizardly {{ill|Oni|en|Oni|qid=Q730052|lt=demon|1=Oni}}, resembling a red-haired, green-skinned humanoid wearing a {{ill|Tiger|en|Tiger|qid=Q19939|lt=Tiger|1=Tiger}} or {{ill|Leopard|en|Leopard|qid=Q34706|lt=Leopard|1=Leopard}} skin {{ill|Loincloth|en|Loincloth|qid=Q1130359|lt=Loincloth|1=Loincloth}}/{{ill|kilt|en|kilt|qid=Q151948|lt=kilt|1=Kilt}}, carrying a large bag of winds (風袋; Kazebuko/Fūtai) on his shoulders. In Japanese art, the deity is often depicted together with [[Raijin]], the god of {{ill|Lightning|en|Lightning|qid=Q33741|lt=Lightning|1=Lightning}}, {{ill|Thunder|en|Thunder|qid=Q83301|lt=Thunder|1=Thunder}} and [[:en:Storms|Storms]]. ==Myths== by {{ill|Tawaraya Sōtatsu|en|Tawaraya Sōtatsu|qid=Q1281252|lt=Tawaraya Sōtatsu|1=Tawaraya Sōtatsu}} (1570–1640)]] {{Expand section|date=April 2018}} === Birth === According to ''[[Kojiki]]'', Fūjin and his brother [[Raijin]] were born from {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} after she died. When Izanagi went down to {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|qid=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}} to retrieve his wife, he saw her as a decaying corpse covered with demons. Izanagi rejected her, making Izanami furious, leading her and a few monsters to chase after Izanagi. Izanagi then blocked the entrance to Yomi. However, a few demons and oni escaped the underworld through a crack in the {{ill|boulder|en|boulder|qid=Q581776|lt=boulder|1=Boulder}}, including Fūjin and his brother Raijin.{{cite web |url=https://mythopedia.com/japanese-mythology/gods/fujin/ |title = Fujin – Mythopedia}} ==Depiction== in Nikkō]] The iconography of Fūjin seems to have its origin in the cultural exchanges along the {{ill|Silk Road|en|Silk Road|qid=Q36288|lt=Silk Road|1=Silk Road}}. Starting with the [[:en:Hellenistic|Hellenistic]] period when {{ill|Ancient Greece|en|Ancient Greece|qid=Q11772|lt=Greece|1=Ancient Greece}} occupied parts of {{ill|Central Asia|en|Central Asia|qid=Q27275|lt=Central Asia|1=Central Asia}} and {{ill|India|en|India|qid=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}, the {{ill|Greek mythology|en|Greek mythology|qid=Q34726|lt=Greek|1=Greek mythology}} wind god [[:en:Boreas (god)|Boreas]] became the god Wardo/Oado in {{ill|Bactria|en|Bactria|qid=Q132646|lt=Bactria|1=Bactria}}n {{ill|Greco-Buddhist art|en|Greco-Buddhist art|qid=Q1008014|lt=Greco-Buddhist art|1=Greco-Buddhist art}}, then a wind deity in China (as seen frescoes of the {{ill|Tarim Basin|en|Tarim Basin|qid=Q216622|lt=Tarim Basin|1=Tarim Basin}}; usually named {{ill|Fengbo (deity)|en|Fengbo (deity)|qid=Q3288593|lt=Feng Bo|1=Fengbo (deity)}}/Feng Po - "Uncle Wind" - among various other names), and finally the Japanese Wind God Fūjin. The wind god kept its symbol, the windbag, and its disheveled appearance throughout this evolution. of {{ill|Gandhara|en|Gandhara|qid=Q213651|lt=Gandhara|1=Gandhara}}), Ha11a, 2nd century.
Mi11le: Wind God from {{ill|Kizil Caves|en|Kizil Caves|qid=Q1234019|lt=Kizil|1=Kizil Caves}}, {{ill|Tarim Basin|en|Tarim Basin|qid=Q216622|lt=Tarim Basin|1=Tarim Basin}}, 7th century.
Right: Japanese Wind God Fujin, 17th century.]] ==See also== * [[:en:Aeolus (Odyssey)|Aeolus (Odyssey)]] * {{ill|Rudra|en|Rudra|qid=Q855310|lt=Rudra|1=Rudra}}, the Vedic wind or storm God * {{ill|Vayu|en|Vayu|qid=Q473731|lt=Vayu|1=Vayu}}, {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|qid=Q9089|lt=Hindu|1=Hinduism}} god of wind == References == {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *{{cite book | last = Boardman | first = John | author-link = John Boardman (art historian) | title = The Diffusion of Classical Art in Antiquity | year = 1994 | publisher = [[Princeton University Press]] | location = Princeton, NJ | isbn = 0-691-03680-2 }} *{{cite book | last = Tokyo Kokuritsu Hakubutsukan; Hyōgo Kenritsu Bijutsukan | title = Alexander the Great : East-West cultural contacts from Greece to Japan | year = 2003 | publisher = Tokyo Kokuritsu Hakubutsukan | location = Tokyo | id = OCLC 53886263 }} * {{cite book | last = Bopearachchi | first = Osmund | author-link = Bopearachchi | title = De l'Indus à l'Oxus, Archéologie de l'Asie Centrale | year = 2003 | publisher = Association imago-musée de Lattes | location = Lattes | language = French | isbn = 2-9516679-2-2 }} *{{cite book | last = Errington | first = Elizabeth |author2= Joe Cribb|author3= Maggie Claringbull|author4= Ancient India and Iran Trust|author4-link= Ancient India and Iran Trust|author5= Fitzwilliam Museum | title = The Crossroads of Asia : transformation in image and symbol in the art of ancient Afghanistan and Pakistan | year = 1992 | publisher = Ancient India and Iran Trust | location = Cambridge | isbn = 0-9518399-1-8 }} === Notes === # {{note|Fujin}} "The Japanese wind god images do not belong to a separate tradition apart from that of their Western counter-parts but share the same origins. ... One of the characteristics of these Far Eastern wind god images is the wind bag held by this god with both hands, the origin of which can be traced back to the shawl or mantle worn by [[:en:Boreas (god)|Boreas]]/ Oado." (Katsumi Tanabe, "Alexander the Great, East-West cultural contacts from Greece to Japan", p21) {{jmyth navbox long}} [[File:Fūjin at Sanjūsangen-dō.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Sculpture of Fūjin from [[Sanjūsangen-dō]] [[File:Wind-God-Fujin-by-Ogata-Korin.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Fūjin depicted on a {{ill|Folding screen|en|Folding screen|qid=Q1144689|lt=Folding screen|1=Folding screen}} [[File:Taiyuin-Fujin-Dsc3719.jpg|thumb|401px|right|Statue at {{ill|Rinnō-ji|en|Rinnō-ji|qid=Q1348249|lt=Taiyū-in|1=Rinnō-ji}} [[File:WindGods.JPG|thumb|center|350px|Iconographical evolution of the Wind God.
Left: Greek wind God ({{ill|Greco-Buddhist art|en|Greco-Buddhist art|qid=Q1008014|lt=Greco-Buddhist art|1=Greco-Buddhist art}} [[wiktionary:風神|風神]] [[wiktionary:風天|風天]] [[en:Fūjin]] [[azb:فوجین]] {{Commons category|Fujin}} [[de:Fūjin]] [[es:Fūjin]] [[fa:فوجین]] [[fi:Fūjin]] [[fr:Fūjin (divinité)]] [[hy:Ֆուջին]] [[id:Fūjin]] [[it:Fūjin]] [[ja:風神]] [[ka:ფუჯინი]] [[mni:ꯐꯨꯖꯤꯟ]] [[pt:Fujin]] [[ru:Фудзин]] [[th:ฟูจิง]] [[tr:Fūjin]] [[uk:Бог вітру (Японія)]] [[vi:Fūjin]] [[zh:風神 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q1483957}} [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wind deities]]
